obligation_text: The quality management system shall include: an accountability framework setting out the responsibilities of the management and other staff with regard to all aspects listed in this paragraph; and a corrective actions and corrective action procedures procedure.
scoring_text: Skill in designing AI governance accountability frameworks, including responsibility assignment for management and staff across all quality management system aspects, and corrective action procedure design and implementation.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in designing AI governance accountability frameworks, including responsibility assignment for management and staff across all quality management system aspects, and corrective action procedure design and implementation.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI governance structures, including model risk management, AI lifecycle oversight, accountability assignment, and the organizational conditions that enable responsible AI deployment at scale.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 17(1)(l-m) (obligation) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-002 is the strongest match (STS: 0.668, cosine: 0.757). Secondary: AG-S-002 (AG, cosine: 0.743), AG-S-004 (AG, cosine: 0.714).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.7/10 (raw 0.668). Shared vocabulary: [accountability, ai, assignment, governance, management]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [across, action, aspects, corrective, design, designing] vs WIDAI terms [conditions, deployment, enable, knowledge, lifecycle, model] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.881 (P=0.879, R=0.883). Balanced P/R — the 27-word competency implication and 25-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.7%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.757 > STS 0.668 (gap +0.089). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [accountability, ai, assignment, governance, management] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 17.5%. Shared tokens: [accountability, ai, assignment, governance, management]. EU AI Act-only: [across, action, aspects, corrective, design, designing]. WIDAI-only: [conditions, deployment, enable, knowledge, lifecycle, model].
